title: SlepianEtc Notes
text: Slepian/DPSS: BOLD band-limited; DPSS maximizes energy concentration target bandwidth/region, giving physics-informed compression denoising versus PCA variance-bases. Two modes Temporal Slepian → LatentNeuroVec: fixed DPSS dictionary B (time × k) derived TR bandwidth; voxel loadings L found projection. Reconstruction: X ≈ B_dpss %*% t(L); automatically removes high-frequency physio noise. Spatiotemporal Graph Slepian → ImplicitLatent: temporal DPSS plus spatial graph eigenmodes (smallest Laplacian eigenvalues) respecting voxel adjacency/ROI topology. Reconstruction: X ≈ B_dpss %*% C_core %*% t(U_graph); strong compression ROIs computationally heavier. DPSS generation (avoid heavy deps): implement tridiagonal eigen-solver RcppEigen. Temporal constructor sketch (R/slepian.R) Graph Slepian sketch (R/slepian_spatial.R): build voxel adjacency → Laplacian; RSpectra::eigs_sym(laplacian, k_space, = "SM") smooth spatial modes B_s; temporal DPSS B_t; core C <- t(B_t) %*% X %*% B_s; decoder multiplies subsets demand. Comparative value: denoising “free” keeping first K = 2NW - 1 tapers; data SVD needed (basis analytic); coefficients may store sparsely; PCA remains general, Haar provides locality, Slepian gives bandwidth-aware frequency interpretability. Roadmap: implement temporal Slepian first; add Rcpp tridiagonal DPSS generator; optional denoise = TRUE drop --band tapers; later add graph Slepian ROI compression.
code: slepian_temporal_latent <- function(X, mask, tr, bandwidth = 0.1, k = NULL) {   n_time <- nrow(X)   W  <- bandwidth * tr           # normalized half-bandwidth   NW <- n_time * W                # time-bandwidth product   if (is.null(k)) k <- max(1, floor(2 * NW) - 1)  # Shannon number    B <- generate_dpss_basis_rcpp(n_time, NW, k)    # orthogonal DPSS basis   L <- Matrix::crossprod(X, B)                    # loadings (voxels × k)    LatentNeuroVec(     basis    = Matrix::Matrix(B),     loadings = Matrix::Matrix(L),     space    = neuroim2::space(mask),     mask     = mask,     offset   = colMeans(X),     meta     = list(family = "slepian_temporal", tr = tr, bandwidth = bandwidth, nw = NW)   ) }

title: SlepianEtc Notes
text: Core hurdle: full-brain spatial Slepian (≈1e5 voxels) naive eigensolve O(N^3); need sparsity/locality tricks. Approach 1 — Cluster-Slepian (recommended): parcel mask (e.g., Schaefer 400 K-means) ~500–1000 clusters; parcel solve tiny Laplacian eigenproblem (k_per_cluster = 1 gives parcel mean; 2–5 adds gradients). Assemble block-diagonal sparse L; temporal basis B <- X %*% L; yields LatentNeuroVec sparse loadings natural “lifting” (voxel signal → smooth parcel modes). Approach 2 — Nyström: pick ~5% voxels landmarks, solve eigenproblem (~5k nodes), extend eigenvectors remaining voxels via Nyström/interpolation (HRBF/linear); optionally threshold sparsify L; keeps smooth whole-brain gradients without hard parcel boundaries. Approach 3 — Tensor-product lattice: assume regular 3D grid; take Kronecker 1D Slepians/DCT along x/y/z, mask non-brain voxels; avoids eigendecomp entirely, extreme speed regular grids. Helper idea: lift_parcellation(parcellation, mask, k=3) builds sparse block-diagonal lifting matrix (per-parcel eigenmodes) reuse across runs.
code: slepian_cluster_latent <- function(X, mask, clusters, k_per_cluster = 3, tr, bw) {   cluster_ids <- sort(unique(clusters[clusters > 0]))   i <- j <- x <- list(); col_off <- 0   for (cid in cluster_ids) {     vox <- which(clusters == cid)     lap <- get_laplacian(get_local_adjacency(mask, vox))      # small (<= few hundred)     k <- min(k_per_cluster, length(vox))     vecs <- RSpectra::eigs_sym(lap, k = k, which = "SM")$vectors     for (cc in seq_len(k)) {       i[[length(i)+1]] <- vox       j[[length(j)+1]] <- rep(col_off + cc, length(vox))       x[[length(x)+1]] <- vecs[, cc]     }     col_off <- col_off + k   }   L <- Matrix::sparseMatrix(i = unlist(i), j = unlist(j), x = unlist(x),                             dims = c(sum(mask), col_off))   B <- as.matrix(X %*% L)   LatentNeuroVec(basis = Matrix::Matrix(B), loadings = L,                  space = neuroim2::space(mask), mask = mask) }

title: SlepianEtc Notes
text: Clustering vs Graph Coarsening: partition (supervoxels) makes Laplacian block-diagonal; coarsening builds multiscale coarse graph plus prolongation P lift eigenvectors (v_fine = P %*% v_coarse). Tradeoffs Clustering: extreme sparsity, embarrassingly parallel tiny eigenproblems, hard boundaries cut inter-parcel edges—great storage/compression; kills global gradients. Coarsening: preserves topology global gradients; lifting via P spreads support → denser loadings, heavier implementation (multigrid/Kron reduction). Fit fmrilatent: prefer supervoxel/cluster Slepians—maximally sparse loadings suit LatentNeuroVec; blockiness mitigated keeping k>1 modes per parcel (mean + gradients). “Soft supervoxel” compromise: keep supervoxel solves allow boundary voxels share weights across adjacent parcels, turning block-diagonal lightly banded loading matrix (smooths seams without dense blow-). Supervoxel constructor sketch Guideline: use clustering compression/denoising pipelines; consider coarsening smooth whole-brain gradients primary added density acceptable.
code: slepian_supervoxel_latent <- function(X, mask, n_supervoxels = 500, k_per_voxel = 3) {   sv <- neuroim2::cluster_voxels(mask, k = n_supervoxels, method = "slic")   i <- j <- x <- list(); col <- 0   for (id in sort(unique(sv[sv > 0]))) {     vox <- which(sv == id)     lap <- get_local_laplacian(mask, vox)     vecs <- RSpectra::eigs_sym(lap, k = min(k_per_voxel, length(vox)), which = "SM")$vectors     for (cc in seq_len(ncol(vecs))) {       i[[length(i)+1]] <- vox       j[[length(j)+1]] <- rep(col + cc, length(vox))       x[[length(x)+1]] <- vecs[, cc]     }     col <- col + ncol(vecs)   }   L <- Matrix::sparseMatrix(i = unlist(i), j = unlist(j), x = unlist(x),                             dims = c(sum(mask), col))   B <- as.matrix(X %*% L)   LatentNeuroVec(basis = B, loadings = L, space = neuroim2::space(mask),                  mask = mask, meta = list(method = "slepian_supervoxel")) }

title: SlepianEtc Notes
text: Goal: general Lifting API decoupling reduction (topology simplification) basis math; plug-architecture Slepians, PCA, flat averaging, etc. Pipeline: Reduce (ambient voxels → reduced graph via clusters/coarsening/landmarks) → Define Basis (e.g., Laplacian eigenvectors, local PCA, identity) → Lift (pull reduced basis back ambient; produce sparse/dense loadings). Classes: abstract GraphReduction (mask, adjacency optional); ClusterReduction (map voxel→cluster, cluster_ids); CoarsenedReduction (prolongation P_matrix, coarse coords). Basis specs light structs: basis_slepian(k,type), basis_pca(k,whiten), basis_flat(). Generic lifter lift(ClusterReduction, spec_slepian): loop clusters, build local adjacency/laplacian, solve k smallest eigs (RSpectra), assemble block-diagonal dgCMatrix. lift(CoarsenedReduction, spec_slepian): solve eigs coarse laplacian, lift via P_matrix %*% V_coarse (denser, band-sparse). Workflow: mask → graph_reduce(..., method="supervoxels"/"coarsen") → spec <- basis_slepian(k=5) → L <- lift(reducer, spec) → LatentNeuroVec(basis = X %*% L, loadings = L, ...). Elegance: separation concerns (topology vs basis vs storage), extensible (add wavelets/new reducers without touching lifter users), defaults return Matrix::sparseMatrix stay memory-light; implicit latents return decoder closures API.
code: setGeneric("lift", function(reduction, basis_spec, data = NULL, ...) standardGeneric("lift"))

title: SlepianEtc Notes
text: Plug-fit current LatentNeuroVec: spatial atoms live loadings, temporal atoms basis; Slepian_space can replace HRBF new class. Pattern: Spatial graph Slepian recipe: build voxel graph; take first K Laplacian eigenvectors (band-limit); solve Slepian concentration ROI get K’ orthonormal atoms concentrated ROI. Orthogonal, bandwidth- ROI-controllable; fewer hyperparams HRBF. Temporal DPSS family: treat 1D Slepians temporal family (meta$family = "dpss_time"). can combine DPSS_time × Slepian_space keeping separable basis[,k] * loadings[,k]—encode pairing meta (time_family, space_family, params). Full joint spatiotemporal Slepians break separability need new class (time_basis, space_basis, coeff grid)—defer future phase. API sketches mirroring HRBF: slepian_space_basis(mask, roi_mask=mask, bandwidth=K, n_slepian=K', laplacian=c("unnormalized","normalized")) -> atoms x vox slepian_project_matrix(X, mask, roi_mask, params) -> time x atoms slepian_latent(X, mask, roi_mask=mask, params, label="") returns LatentNeuroVec tagged family="slepian_space". Helpers: as_slepian_latent(), is_slepian_latent(), slepian_params(). Practical checklist: make_voxel_graph(mask, connectivity=6/18/26, weight_scheme="binary"|"distance"). slepian_space_basis(...) (store eigenvalues/concentration). slepian_project_matrix(...) (time × atoms). Temporal DPSS: dpss_time_basis(T, nw, K); optional as_dpss_time_latent. Mixing helpers combine/compare HRBF vs Slepian vs DPSS latents using meta tags.
code: B_atoms_vox <- slepian_space_basis(mask, roi_mask, params)   # atoms x vox coeff       <- slepian_project_matrix(X, mask, roi_mask, params) # time x atoms loadings    <- Matrix::t(B_atoms_vox) LatentNeuroVec(basis = coeff, loadings = loadings,                space = neuroim2::NeuroSpace(...),                mask = mask, meta = list(family = "slepian_space", params = params))

title: SlepianEtc Notes
text: Core mantra: reduction → basis → lift → latent; Slepian one basis choice, bolt-. Keep single latent shapes (LatentNeuroVec; ImplicitLatent core tensor makes sense) separate topology math; default sparse loadings. Layered architecture Layer 0 primitives: DPSS generator RcppEigen generate_dpss_basis_rcpp(N, NW, K) R wrapper dpss_time_basis(n_time, tr, bandwidth, k=NULL). Layer 1 engine: GraphReduction subclasses (ClusterReduction map voxel→cluster; CoarsenedReduction P_matrix), basis_* specs (basis_slepian, basis_pca, basis_flat), lift() generic. Cluster+Slepian → block-sparse L; Coarsen+Slepian → denser via P; Cluster+PCA → data-driven local basis. User workflows Temporal DPSS (Mode ): slepian_temporal_latent(X, mask, tr, bandwidth=0.1, k=NULL, denoise=TRUE) builds DPSS B, L = X’ %*% B, returns LatentNeuroVec meta {family=“slepian_temporal”, tr, bandwidth, nw}. Spectral supervoxel Slepian (Mode B default spatial): slepian_supervoxel_latent(X, mask, n_supervoxels=500, k_per_voxel=3) → reduction=supervoxels, spec=basis_slepian, L = lift(…), B = X %*% L, LatentNeuroVec meta {family=“slepian_supervoxel”}; columns: parcel mean + gradients. Spatiotemporal: combine DPSS_time × Slepian_space. Simple path keeps separable LatentNeuroVec (pairing encoded meta). Clean path uses ImplicitLatent core C = t(B_t) %% X %% L_s, time_basis = B_t, space_basis = L_s, meta tags {time_family=“slepian_temporal”, space_family=“slepian_supervoxel”}. Official stance whole-brain eigensolves: default cluster/supervoxel reductions scalability sparsity; offer CoarsenedReduction explicitly global gradients (warn denser loadings); full whole-brain Slepians --scope now. Roadmap Phase 1: Temporal DPSS (C++ gen, slepian_temporal_latent, tests/vignette vs PCA). Phase 2: Reduction + lift API (GraphReduction, graph_reduce, basis specs, lift; ClusterReduction + basis_slepian path working). Phase 3: Spectral supervoxel Slepian (k_per_voxel/n_supervoxels knobs, denoise/compression controls). Phase 4: Spatiotemporal ImplicitLatent (B_t, L_s, C) benchmarks. Phase 5: Optional graph coarsening path (advanced/experimental).

title: Encoding API reference
text: encode() primary entry point. takes: x: data matrix (time × voxels mask order), NeuroVec object spec: spec object describing basis family mask: LogicalNeuroVol logical array (required spatial spatiotemporal specs) reduction: optional GraphReduction spatial specs (built automatically omitted) materialize: "handle" (lazy, default) "matrix" (eager) label: optional string label function dispatches x spec, selecting appropriate encoding algorithm.

title: Encoding API reference
text: reduce time dimension. data matrix X projected onto temporal basis B, yielding loadings L = t(X) %*% B (voxels × k). Slepian/DPSS — Discrete prolate spheroidal sequences concentrate energy frequency band minimizing spectral leakage. tr: repetition time seconds bandwidth: half-bandwidth Hz (default 0.1) k: number tapers (default: floor(2 * n * bandwidth * tr) - 1) backend: "tridiag" (default) "dense" DCT — discrete cosine transform provides orthogonal frequency components. k: number components norm: "ortho" (default) "none" B-spline — Smooth, localized basis functions controllable flexibility. k: number basis functions (degrees freedom) degree: spline degree (default 3, .e., cubic) include_intercept: include constant term (default FALSE) orthonormalize: orthonormalize columns via QR (default TRUE)
code: spec_time_slepian(tr = 2, bandwidth = 0.08, k = 4) spec_time_dct(k = 10, norm = "ortho") spec_time_bspline(k = 8, degree = 3, orthonormalize = TRUE)

title: Encoding API reference
text: reduce voxel dimension. loadings matrix L (voxels × k) defines spatial atoms; basis becomes B = X %*% L (time × k). Graph Slepian — Eigenvectors graph Laplacian, concentrated spatial regions. k: components per cluster k_neighbors: neighbors k-NN graph construction Heat wavelet — Polynomial approximation heat diffusion voxel graph. scales: diffusion time scales order: Chebyshev polynomial order threshold: coefficient truncation threshold HRBF — Hierarchical radial basis functions Gaussian kernels. params: list controlling atom placement shape (see ?hrbf_generate_basis) Wavelet active — CDF 5/3 lifting wavelets operate -mask voxels. levels_space: spatial decomposition levels levels_time: optional temporal lifting levels threshold: coefficient thresholding
code: spec_space_slepian(k = 3, k_neighbors = 6) spec_space_heat(scales = c(1, 2, 4, 8), order = 30, threshold = 1e-6, k_neighbors = 6) spec_space_hrbf(params = list(sigma0 = 2, levels = 1, radius_factor = 2.5, kernel_type = "gaussian")) spec_space_wavelet_active(levels_space = 2, levels_time = 0, threshold = 0)

title: Encoding API reference
text: spec_st() combines temporal spatial spec separable representation. Instead storing full reconstructed matrix, stores core tensor decodes demand. result ImplicitLatent object. Call decoder time_idx /roi_mask partial reconstruction.

title: Diffusion Wavelets (Section 2.3 & Figure 2)
text: Goal: build data-adaptive multiscale bases graph/manifold diffusing Markov operator T compressing range dyadic powers filter high-frequency detail. Dyadic powers: examine T^(2^j); j grows, diffusion spreads numerical rank drops. Compression/basis update: scale, run modified, rank-revealing QR current operator representation find coarse orthonormal scaling basis phi_{j+1} within tolerance epsilon. Scaling functions stored “extended” back original basis phi_0. Wavelets orthogonal complement. Downsampling operator: project/square compressed operator form T^(2^{j+1}) new basis, iterating space collapses (rank 1 stopping rule).

title: Diffusion Wavelets (Section 2.3 & Figure 2)
code: def DiffusionWavelets(T, epsilon):     # Inputs: T (N x N transition/similarity matrix), epsilon (compression precision)     # Outputs: extended scaling bases phis, extended wavelet bases psis (each in terms of phi_0)     phi_0 = I_N                          # Dirac basis on original nodes     current_T = T                        # [T]_phi0^phi0     extended_phis = [phi_0]              # [phi_j]_phi0     extended_psis = []     j = 0     while size(current_T) > 1:           # or other stop (rank collapse / max_J)         Q, R = ModifiedQR(current_T, epsilon)        # Q ~ [phi_{j+1}]_phi_j ; R ~ [T^(2^j)]_phi_j^phi_{j+1}         phi_next_global = Q @ extended_phis[j]       # [phi_{j+1}]_phi0         extended_phis.append(phi_next_global)          Q_wavelet, _ = ModifiedQR(I - Q @ Q.T, epsilon)  # wavelet complement in phi_j coordinates         psi_next_global = Q_wavelet @ extended_phis[j]   # [psi_j]_phi0         extended_psis.append(psi_next_global)          compressed_T = R @ Q                          # [T^(2^j)]_phi_{j+1}^phi_{j+1}         current_T = compressed_T @ compressed_T       # square for dyadic step: [T^(2^{j+1})]_phi_{j+1}^phi_{j+1}         j += 1     return extended_phis, extended_psis

title: Diffusion Wavelets (Section 2.3 & Figure 2)
text: Bottleneck: deterministic rank-revealing QR dense N x N matrices O(N^3) diffusion causes fill-. Intractable many-thousand–million node graphs. Remedy: randomized rank-revealing QR (Halko–Martinsson–Tropp style) approximate column space factoring. Draw Gaussian Omega shape n x (k+p). Form sketch Y = @ Omega (fast sparse/implicit ). Orthonormalize Y -> Q = qr(Y). Project small matrix B = Q.T @ . Factor small B deterministic QR/SVD: U_small, R_small = qr(B). Reconstruct basis Q_final = Q @ U_small, giving ≈ Q_final @ R_small. Complexity drops toward O(N * k^2) O(N^2 log k), heavy lift highly parallelizable multiply @ Omega.

title: Diffusion Wavelets (Section 2.3 & Figure 2)
text: Short answer: vanilla DWT (basic randomized QR) scale 100K-node small-world connectome unless extra constraints added. Fill-small-world topology: degree ~100 gives ~10K neighbors T^2 near-full coverage T^4, making T^(2^j) dense (~80 GB 100K x 100K) QR O(1e15) FLOPs. Slow spectral decay: connectome spectra decay slowly, epsilon-driven truncation may still require tens thousands basis vectors (e.g., 40K), making QR 100K x 40K matrix infeasible. Constraints make workable - Never form T^2 explicitly: evaluate products Y = T(T Omega) cost scales nnz(T) rather N^2. - Aggressive sparsification: threshold small entries (e.g., < 1e-5) compression prevent density growth. - Fix target rank (e.g., 2K–5K) instead epsilon-based rank discovery; accept loss high-frequency detail stay tractable. Sketch scalable loop (brain-sized) Effect: Figure 2 flow blow first squaring; constrained randomized path can finish realistic hardware capping rank keeping operations implicit.
code: def Scalable_Brain_DWT(T_sparse, target_rank=2000, oversample=20, max_scales=...):     current_T = T_sparse  # 100K x 100K sparse (~10M nnz)     for _ in range(max_scales):         Omega = randn(current_T.shape[0], target_rank + oversample)         Y = current_T.dot(current_T.dot(Omega))          # implicit T^2 @ Omega, sparse-dense         Q, _ = np.linalg.qr(Y, mode="reduced")           # tall-skinny QR         B = current_T.dot(Q)                             # sparse-dense multiply         T_compressed = B.T.dot(B)                        # small dense (target_rank x target_rank)         T_compressed[np.abs(T_compressed) < 1e-5] = 0    # enforce sparsity if desired         save_basis(Q)                                    # store basis analog of [phi_{j+1}]_phi_j         current_T = T_compressed         if current_T.shape[0] < target_rank:             break

title: Diffusion Wavelets (Section 2.3 & Figure 2)
text: Works raw voxel graphs (100K+ nodes) using implicit, capped-rank path ; sparsity thresholding essential. Directly usable reduced/clustered/coarsened graphs; smaller N eases rank cap may allow epsilon-driven truncation. Bases compressed operators can composed across resolutions: run DWT coarse graph, lift restrict via cluster assignments mixing methods (e.g., spectral embeddings, HRBF, GCNs). randomized, implicit multiplies mean code path can operate graph representation long T provided sparse/linear operator.

title: Diffusion Wavelets (Section 2.3 & Figure 2)

Operator interface: accept T Matrix function f(x) returning T %*% x; never materialize T^2. Provide helper implicit_power_mult(T_op, X) evaluate T(T X). Core routine: implement randomized_diffusion_step(T_op, target_rank, oversample, threshold) returning (Q, T_compressed) using implicit two-pass multiply Y = T(T Omega), tall-skinny QR, small T_compressed = B^T B; include optional thresholding T_compressed. Integration reductions: add lift method lift(ClusterReduction, spec_diffusion_wavelet, ...) builds T_op cluster graph (raw voxel graph via voxel_subset_to_gsp / adjacency), produces stacked scaling functions [phi_{j+1}]_phi0 analogous basis lifts. text: package requires neuroim2 neuroimaging data structures:
code: remotes::install_github("bbuchsbaum/neuroim2")

title: Latent Space Representations of fMRI Data
code: library(fmrilatent) library(neuroim2) library(Matrix)  # Create example data n_time <- 100 n_components <- 10 n_voxels <- 1000  # Temporal basis (time x components) basis <- Matrix(rnorm(n_time * n_components), nrow = n_time)  # Spatial loadings (voxels x components) loadings <- Matrix(rnorm(n_voxels * n_components), nrow = n_voxels, sparse = TRUE)  # Create 4D space and mask space <- NeuroSpace(c(10, 10, 10, n_time)) mask <- LogicalNeuroVol(array(TRUE, c(10, 10, 10)), NeuroSpace(c(10, 10, 10)))  # Construct LatentNeuroVec lvec <- LatentNeuroVec(   basis = basis,   loadings = loadings,   space = space,   mask = mask )  # Access components basis(lvec)      # Get temporal basis loadings(lvec)   # Get spatial loadings lvec[[1]]        # Extract first volume

title: Latent Space Representations of fMRI Data
text: encode() function provides high-level interface creating latent representations:
code: # Create data matrix (time x voxels) X <- matrix(rnorm(n_time * n_voxels), nrow = n_time)  # Encode with temporal Slepian basis lvec <- encode(X, spec_time_slepian(tr = 2, bandwidth = 0.1), mask = mask)  # Encode with DCT basis lvec_dct <- encode(X, spec_time_dct(k = 15), mask = mask)  # Spatiotemporal encoding (separable) lvec_st <- encode(X,    spec_st(     time = spec_time_bspline(k = 10),     space = spec_space_slepian(k = 3)   ),   mask = mask )

title: Latent Space Representations of fMRI Data
text: Temporal bases: - spec_time_slepian() - DPSS/Slepian sequences (bandlimited) - spec_time_dct() - Discrete cosine transform - spec_time_bspline() - B-spline basis Spatial bases: - spec_space_slepian() - Graph Laplacian eigenvectors - spec_space_heat() - Heat/diffusion wavelets - spec_space_hrbf() - Hierarchical radial basis functions Combined: - spec_st() - Separable spatiotemporal (time × space)

title: Latent Space Representations of fMRI Data
text: memory efficiency, bases can computed lazily:
code: # Create with handle (lazy evaluation) lvec <- encode(X, spec_time_slepian(tr = 2), mask = mask, materialize = "handle")  # Basis is computed on first access b <- basis(lvec)  # Materializes and caches the basis

title: Latent Space Representations of fMRI Data
text: Memory efficient: Store factorized representation instead full 4D array Lazy evaluation: Compute bases -demand caching Multiple basis families: Temporal, spatial, spatiotemporal decompositions neuroim2 integration: Works standard neuroimaging data structures Sparse support: Efficient handling sparse loadings matrices

title: HierarchicalBasisTemplate Class — HierarchicalBasisTemplate-class
text: template-, data-agnostic container hierarchical Laplacian frames. Stores sparse spatial dictionary (primal basis), cached solver Gram matrix, parcellation hierarchy metadata. Intended built offline fixed template (e.g., MNI) reused encoding fMRI data.

title: HierarchicalBasisTemplate Class — HierarchicalBasisTemplate-class
text: mask LogicalNeuroVol defining domain (3D). space NeuroSpace (typically 4D singleton time dim) matching mask. levels List integer vectors (one per level) giving parcel ids per voxel (mask order). parents List mapping child parcel ids parent ids level > 1. loadings Sparse Matrix (voxels x atoms) containing concatenated atoms B. gram_factor Cached factorization \(G = t(B) \%*\% B\) (e.g., dCHMsimpl Matrix::Cholesky). atoms data.frame describing atom (col_id, level, parcel_id, parent_id, mode, label). meta List auxiliary metadata (atlas names, k_per_level, ridge, version).

title: LatentNeuroVec Class — LatentNeuroVec-class
text: class represents 4-dimensional neuroimaging array using latent space decomposition. stores data set basis functions (dictionary) corresponding set loadings (coefficients), enabling efficient representation manipulation high-dimensional data.

title: LatentNeuroVec Class — LatentNeuroVec-class
text: LatentNeuroVec inherits NeuroVec-class AbstractSparseNeuroVec-class. original 4D data can reconstructed : $$data[v,t] = \sum_k \bigl(basis[t,k] \times loadings[v,k]\bigr) + offset[v]$$ v indexes voxels within mask t indexes time points. approach especially useful large datasets storing full 4D array expensive. Common use cases include: PCA-reduced fMRI data ICA decompositions Dictionary learning representations matrix factorization neuroimaging data

title: LatentNeuroVec Class — LatentNeuroVec-class
text: basis Matrix BasisHandle column represents basis vector latent space. Dimensions (nTime x k) k number components. loadings Matrix LoadingsHandle (often sparse) containing coefficients basis vector across spatial dimensions. Dimensions (nVoxels x k). offset numeric vector representing constant offset term voxel spatial location. Length nVoxels (within mask). map IndexLookupVol object representing mapping 3D coordinates linear indices within mask. label character string representing label latent vector. meta list storing lightweight metadata (e.g., HRBF params, centers).

title: LatentNeuroVec Class — LatentNeuroVec-class

text: mask LogicalNeuroVol defining 3D brain mask (MNI space). parcels Integer. Number Schaefer parcels (100, 200, 300, 400, 500, 600, 800, 1000). networks Integer. Yeo network variant (7 17). space Character. Surface space ("fsaverage6", "fsaverage", "fsaverage5"). k_levels Integer vector cluster counts coarser levels, ordered coarse→fine. finest level always original Schaefer parcellation. Example: c(16, 32, 64) produces 4 levels: L0=16, L1=32, L2=64, L3=Schaefer-parcels. k_per_level Integer vector eigenmodes per parcel level. Length must match length(k_levels) + 1 (finest Schaefer level). vol_atlas Optional. Pre-loaded volumetric Schaefer atlas (NeuroVol parcel IDs). NULL, attempts load via neuroatlas::get_schaefer_atlas(). alpha Weight boundary contact similarity (default 0.5). beta Weight geodesic kernel similarity (default 0.3). gamma Weight Yeo network agreement similarity (default 0.2). d0 Scale geodesic exponential kernel (default 30 mm). component_policy handle fragmented parcels ("error", "largest", "", "merge"). cache Logical. Use cached geodesic distances available (default TRUE). k_neighbors k local graph construction inside parcels. ridge Small diagonal ridge Gram matrix stability. solver Solver choice: "chol" "qr".

what: Build hierarchical parcellation levels from Schaefer surface atlas — build_schaefer_levels
title: Build hierarchical parcellation levels from Schaefer surface atlas — build_schaefer_levels
text: Constructs nested parcellation levels hierarchical template : 1. Loading Schaefer surface atlas neuroatlas 2. Computing geodesic distances boundary contacts via neurosurf 3. Building parcel similarity matrix (geodesic + boundary + network) 4. Performing spectral Ward clustering create coarser levels 5. Mapping surface parcels back volumetric voxel labels Real-data glue finish: - Provide fsaverage Schaefer surfaces (via `neuroatlas::schaefer_surf`) can compute geodesic/boundary terms. - Provide volumetric Schaefer atlas aligned MNI mask (via `neuroatlas::get_schaefer_atlas`). - Ensure mask volumetric atlas share resolution/origin/spacing; function assumes 2 mm MNI unless pass custom `vol_atlas`. - Subcortex **** handled ; add separately assembling full template.

what: fmrilatent: Latent Space Representations of fMRI Data — fmrilatent-package
title: fmrilatent: Latent Space Representations of fMRI Data — fmrilatent-package
text: Provides S4 classes methods representing neuroimaging data latent spaces. LatentNeuroVec class offers memory-efficient representation using matrix factorization (basis x loadings), enabling compact storage efficient computation dimensionality-reduced fMRI data PCA ICA decompositions. Integrates neuroim2 package standard neuroimaging data structures.
